compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: 3 independent reviewer subagents reviewed the on-ramp docs (questions: what's still ambiguous about creating a passing plan; what's missing). Refining docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md on the 7 items ALL THREE independently surfaced (avoiding overfit to any single reviewer): (1) leaf-vs-container determination; (2) grounding citation format + how to list not-yet-existing files in the edit-set; (3) what stales an attestation; (4) review-plan-vs-check-ac/clarity-check gate relationship; (5) a worked minimum-viable passing plan example; (6) what a review-plan finding looks like + recovery; (7) per-type section applicability. Dropping items only 2/3 raised (--force consequences, project-configurability, exact heading matching).

compiled_state.description:
## Context / Problem

Agents and contributors struggle to discover *how to write a plan that passes the
plan-review gate*. The existing material is either gate-mechanics (`plan-review-gate.md`) or a
generated, reviewer-facing criterion registry (`plan-review-criteria-guide.md` / `rebar
explain <id>`) — there is no concise, author-facing on-ramp, and AGENTS.md mispoints authors
at the criterion registry for the "description template". The same discoverability gap exists
for the code-review (`LLM-Review` + `Verified`) gate.

## Approach

Add two hand-written, prose on-ramp docs — `docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` and
`docs/passing-code-review.md` — kept short and distinct from the generated registry (so the
registry stays the single source of truth for the reviewer). Make them discoverable from the
CLI/MCP by extending the existing `rebar explain` seam with author-guide names (`rebar explain
plan` / `rebar explain review`) via the one shared `registry` lookup that already backs the
CLI, the MCP `explain_criterion` tool, and the library. Fix the AGENTS.md pointers. Rejected
alternative: generating these from the registry (rejected — the on-ramp is prose, not a
per-criterion rubric, and would fight the parity gate).

## Scope

- docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md (new), docs/passing-code-review.md (new)
- docs/README.md (link both new docs — the docs-index CI gate requires every top-level
  docs/*.md to be linked from the index)
- AGENTS.md (fix pointers; add code-review on-ramp reference)
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py (AUTHOR_GUIDES + explain_guide)
- src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py (_explain routes guide names)
- src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py (explain_criterion routes guide names)
- tests/unit/test_plan_review_explain.py (cover the guide path)

## Testing

Unit tests over the new `explain_guide` path across all three surfaces (library, CLI, MCP):
success for every registered guide, unknown-name → `unknown-id`, missing-file → `missing-file`.
Pure in-process logic (no live boundary), so offline unit coverage exercises the real path.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` exists and states the description template + the
      blocking-criteria checklist; verified by reading the file and `rebar explain plan`
      printing it (exit 0).
- [ ] `docs/passing-code-review.md` exists covering the commit checklist, LLM-Review dimensions
      (blocking vs advisory), and vote responses; `rebar explain review` prints it (exit 0).
- [ ] `rebar explain <criterion-id>` still works unchanged; an unknown topic exits 1 with a
      structured `unknown-id` error.
- [ ] AGENTS.md points authors at both new docs; no pointer claims the template lives in the
      criterion registry.
- [ ] Both new docs are linked from `docs/README.md`, so the docs-index CI gate stays green;
      verified by `pytest tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py::test_current_repo_index_is_clean`.
- [ ] `make lint` and the explain unit tests pass.

file_impact.reason: link the two new docs so test_check_docs_index passes
fix pointers; add code-review on-ramp reference
explain_criterion MCP tool routes guide names

compiled_state.title: Author-facing plan-review & code-review on-ramp docs + rebar explain plan/review
